explanation:
There are two requirements: (1) In PSV mode, min_exp_time_psv is always equal to half of the last inspiration time, and (2) min_exp_time_psv must always remain within the interval [0.4, 2] seconds. 

(1) For the first part, we interpret 'in PSV mode' as the atomic proposition PSVMode being true. 'min_exp_time_psv shall be the half of the last inspiration time' corresponds to the atomic proposition minExpiratoryTime_equalTo_inspClock_divideBy_2 being true, since this indicates the requirement holds. We want this to be true whenever PSVMode is true (i.e., globally, if PSVMode, then minExpiratoryTime_equalTo_inspClock_divideBy_2). 

(2) For the second part, we assume the atomic propositions minExpiratoryTime_greaterThanOrEqual_0_4 and minExpiratoryTime_lessThanOrEqual_2 mean min_exp_time_psv is at least 0.4 seconds and at most 2 seconds, respectively. We need these to be true any time PSVMode is active. So, globally, if PSVMode, then both are true: PSVMode -> (minExpiratoryTime_greaterThanOrEqual_0_4 & minExpiratoryTime_lessThanOrEqual_2). 

We then combine both requirements conjunctively: globally, if we are in PSVMode, then both (a) min_exp_time_psv equals half of last inspiration time, and (b) min_exp_time_psv in [0.4,2].

Thus, the complete LTL formula: G(PSVMode -> (minExpiratoryTime_equalTo_inspClock_divideBy_2 & minExpiratoryTime_greaterThanOrEqual_0_4 & minExpiratoryTime_lessThanOrEqual_2)).
